title: rosa eglanteria var. sepium
intro: Rosa eglanteria var. sepium, also known as the wild briar or dog rose, is a perennial shrub native to Europe and parts of Asia, characterized by its thorny stems and fragrant flowers. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it useful in treating wounds and skin conditions such as eczema and acne. The health benefits of Rosa eglanteria var. sepium include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate digestive issues, and support cardiovascular health, making it a popular herbal remedy in traditional medicine. Rosa eglanteria var. sepium can be prepared as a herbal tea, tincture, or infused oil, which can be used topically or taken internally to harness its medicinal properties.
